Greg 19 Mar

SIP would like to engage agencies for video production Zhang Yu 14 Mar
(weekly email)

shipping Nicholas 14 Mar (weekly email)

Shuning 17 Mar by email

with counter TB SKUs (not only TB sellers) Nicholas 14 Mar (weekly email)

version submitted to CB commercial) Steven Immediate

Following up on Action item #1 from the March SIP Monthly meeting, please
find the SIP CS Plan in the attached PDF.

Thanks

content.question:
Thanks.



+@Eric Bui <eric.bui@shopee.com>



Slide 2

   - How does the reporting line work right now?

I think that needs to be
   fixed regardless of what option we choose. The business team managing it
   has to be in charged of the team of agents.



Slide 4

   - To handle SIP chats, what language proficiency does 1 require?

- For the long term, I am not sure we considered things like language,
   ability to hire etc?

I am not convinced with our long term recommendation
   in general
   - For the short- term, what do we need to fix?

Slide 5

   - Not quite sure what we are going to do and when?

Especially for the
   chatbot support for the 5 key lanes..
   - And what is the expectation over time in terms of CSAT, Deflection
   Rate, Conversion improvement?

Hi Ian，

Slide 2

   -

   How does the reporting line work right now? I think that needs to be
   fixed regardless of what option we choose. The business team managing it
   has to be in charge of the team of agents.
   -

      As-is reporting situation
      -

         Currently, agents handling SIP chats report to local operations,
         not SIP. Current reporting structure: Local Market Agent >
Local Team Leads
         -

         SIP does not have direct authority over agent management but only
         oversees SIP CS performance


   -

   To be: SIP to consider:
   -

      Agent location: 1) consolidate into 1 location vs 2-3 locations; 2)
      location choice (e.g., ID/PH/MY, etc.)
      -

      Agent reporting line: report to SIP OPS team
      -

   Next Step: work with Reg ops / local ops team on plan with
   considerations: (ETA: mid April to update in sip monthly meeting)
   -

      Agent availability / challenge to hire / local policy risks
      -

      Agent quality/language proficiency
      -

      Cost/efficiency impact
      -

      Team management considerations


Slide 4

   -

   To handle SIP chats, what language proficiency does 1 require?
   -

      Buyer language in each A market
      -

         SG: 100% English
         -

         MY:  50% Malay, 50% English
         -

         Other markets (VN/TH/PH/TW/Latam): local language
         -

      On top of hiring agents proficient in above languages, we have also
      existing/upcoming translation tool to support
      -

         Existing tool (for CB SCS): translate between local language and
         English, CB SCS CSAT 70%+, to support agents speaking
English/local language
         -

         Upcoming tool: translate between local language and
         English/Bahasa, which can enable hiring extension to agents
speaking Bahasa
         [ETA: Q3]



   -

   For the long term, I

content.question: Can come back with a better end to end proposal?

Problems, Short term fix,
long term fix, how we are tracking it etc.



Right now, I am not convinced that we thought this through properly..



When can this be done?

*From:* Wei Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Monday, 24 March 2025 8:24 pm
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Eric Bui <eric.bui@shopee.com>; Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>;
Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <
shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Keljin Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Yu Zhang <
yu.zhang@shopee.com>; Steven Liu <steven.liusk@shopee.com>; Nicholas Lim <
nicholas.lim@shopee.com>; Huang Wan-Jung <wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>; Joe
Li <joe.liyc@shopee.com>; Ren Jiahui <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>; Jason Yu <
jason.yu@shopee.com>; Grzegorz Koblanski <greg.koblanski@shopee.com>;
Ashley Liang <ashley.liangsq@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: Mar SIP monthly meeting - pre-reads



Hi Ian，



Slide 2

   - How does the reporting line work right now?

I think that needs to be
   fixed regardless of what option we choose. The business team managing it
   has to be in charge of the team of agents.


   - *As-is* reporting situation


   - Currently, agents handling SIP chats report to local operations, not
         SIP. Current reporting structure: Local Market Agent > Local
Team Leads >
         Local Market M/SPV > Local Market Ops Head
         - SIP does not have direct authority over agent management but
         only oversees SIP CS performance

·       *To be*: SIP to consider:

o   *Agent location*: 1) consolidate into 1 location vs 2-3 locations; 2)
location choice (e.g., ID/PH/MY, etc.)

o   *Agent reporting* *line*: report to SIP OPS team

·       *Next Step*: work with Reg ops / local ops team on plan with
considerations: (ETA: mid April to update in sip monthly meeting)

o   Agent availability / challenge to hire / local policy risks

o   Agent quality/language proficiency

o   Cost/efficiency impact

o   Team management considerations



Slide 4

   - To handle SIP chats, what language proficiency does 1 require?

- Buyer language in each A market


   - SG: 100% English
         - MY:  50% Malay, 50% English
         - Other markets (VN/TH/PH/TW/Latam): local language


   - On top of hiring agents proficient in above languages, we have also
      existing/upcoming translation tool to support


   - Existing tool (for CB SCS): translate between local language and
         English, CB SCS CSAT 70%+, to support agents speaking
English/local language
         - Upcoming tool: translate between local language and
         English/Bahasa, which can enable hiring extension to agents
speaking Bahasa
         [ETA: Q3]



   - For the long term, I am not sure we considered things like language,
   ability to hire etc?

I am not convinced with our long term recommendation
   in general


   - Noted. We will come back on long-term plan as mentioned above in the
      1st question


   - For the short- term, what do we need to fix?

- In the short term, we will address key issues affecting agent
      performance, including CSAT, productivity, and abandonment rate, by CS
      location markets, covering top 5 lanes

§  All import lanes to SG + all import lanes to MY (including MYSG, VNMY):
improve CSAT [ETA 10 Apr]

§  All import lanes to PH: Enhance productivity [ETA 3 Apr]

§  All export lanes from ID (including IDSG, IDMY, IDPH): Reduce high
abandonment rate [ETA 11 Apr]



Slide 5

   - Not quite sure what we are going to do and when?

Especially for the
   chatbot support for the 5 key lanes..


   - We will roll out NLU+LLM model to top 5 lanes and all SIP lanes in 4
      phases


   - *Mid April:* conduct A/B testing vs current NLU model for all mkts
         - *End April:* Fix issues during A/B testing, roll out the new
         model to all shops across all SIP lanes
         - *Q2*: upgrade SIP chatbot to include upselling and cross-selling
         functionalities



   - And what is the expectation over time in terms of CSAT, Deflection
   Rate, Conversion improvement?
